<!--
Organization-wide template. Do not edit this file in place for a specific milestone.

How to use:
1. Copy this file into your project repository.
2. Save it as docs/milestones/<milestone-slug>.md using a lowercase kebab-case,
   behavior-oriented slug (for example: local-project-roots, validate-desired-state).
3. Replace placeholders and remove this comment block.
4. Create a matching GitHub Milestone and link back to this document.
5. Open the first milestone issue: Create Spec Kit spec, plan, and tasks for
   <milestone name>.

Actual milestone definitions live in each project repository, not in the org
.github repository.

For workflow context, see the PathAble Open Source Operating Model:
https://github.com/PathableAI-org/.github/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#open-source-operating-model

Naming: docs/milestones/<milestone-slug>.md
-->

# <Milestone name>

**Release:** [docs/releases/<release-slug>.md](../../releases/<release-slug>.md)

One GitHub Milestone should correspond to this document. The GitHub Milestone
description should link here and to the parent release definition. GitHub
Milestones track issues; this document carries the planning intent and
acceptance contract.
